preamble: A direct shift from the voiced stop [d] to the voiceless sibilant [s] is usually the result of total assimilation in consonant clusters or a complex path of palatalization and affrication.

examples.note: When a stem ending in [d] met a suffix starting with [t], the cluster assibilated to [ss] and simplified to [s].

sources: Weiss: Outline of the Historical and Comparative Grammar of Latin
